title: 7B+ Cross-Paradigm Orthogonality Resolution
hypothesis: The negative surgery x jitter interaction detected at 7B+ in exp-014 (OR=0.92, p=0.013) is architecture-specific heterogeneity concentrated in large transformers, not a universal scale law. SSM/hybrid/RWKV 7B+ models will show interaction near zero (within SESOI), while transformers will remain negative. The permutation p-value conflict (0.35 vs 0.013) is a low-independent-model-count artifact that resolves with a larger panel.
value_to_investors: Determines whether deployment testing at 7B+ scale requires joint geometric-dynamic validation for ALL paradigms (expensive) or only for transformers (targeted). Resolves the exp-014 ambiguity before it propagates into deployment protocols.
value_to_researchers: Tests external validity of the exp-014 breakthrough across 4 paradigms. Introduces Cochran's Q heterogeneity test and random-effects analysis. Updates scale-moderation meta-regression with 4 pooled experiments (012-015). Directly challenges or confirms the most significant finding in the project.
identification_strategy: Same 2x2 factorial as exp-014 (surgery=0.08, jitter=0.08, mid-layer=0.5) held constant for direct comparability. Panel expanded to 10 models across 4 paradigms. 5 seeds (reduced from 7 to allocate budget to more independent models). Exp-014 data reused for 5 completed models. Interaction tested via clustered logistic regression, bootstrap, permutation, LOO, LOPO, and Cochran's Q heterogeneity.
falsification_tests: If pooled interaction returns to ROPE (fraction >= 0.95), exp-014 was a false positive
If SSM/hybrid show interaction magnitude comparable to transformers, it IS a universal scale effect
If permutation p < 0.05 with expanded panel, the p-value conflict was due to low model count
Calibration-holdout gap > 5pp would indicate prompt-level overfitting
success_criteria: >=8 models completed, with >=1 SSM/RWKV and >=1 hybrid
Clear verdict: ORTHOGONALITY_RESTORED, TRANSFORMER_SPECIFIC, or INTERACTION_CONFIRMED
Permutation and model-based p-values directionally consistent (conflict resolved)
Heterogeneity test (Cochran's Q) quantifies between-paradigm variation
Scale-moderation meta-regression updated with exp-015 data

data_lineage.provenance: 10 models at 7B+ across 4 paradigms (transformer, SSM, hybrid, reasoning), NF4 quantized via bitsandbytes on RTX 5090 Laptop (25.7GB VRAM). 5 completed models imported from exp-014.
data_lineage.transformations: Forward pass with hook-based hidden-state perturbation at mid-layer, greedy decoding (max_new_tokens=10), strict answer parsing with numeric extraction

out_of_sample_validation_plan: 32 calibration prompts used for analysis tuning; 32 holdout prompts reserved for final validation. All statistics computed on both splits with divergence check.
uncertainty_methods: Bootstrap CIs (5000 resamples over model-level means)
Permutation null distribution (5000 shuffles of surgery/jitter labels within model)
ROPE equivalence testing with pre-specified bounds [0.90, 1.11]
Cochran's Q heterogeneity test with I-squared and tau-squared
robustness_checks: Paradigm-specific interaction analysis (transformer vs SSM vs hybrid vs reasoning)
Main effects analysis (surgery and jitter independently)
Completion gate check (>=8 models, >=1 SSM, >=1 hybrid)
